Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: pulpitis (K04.005).
Examination: Tooth 32 is temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 32 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Tooth 32: isolation, removal of temporary seal, removal of medicament, access preparation, exploration of one canal, incomplete pulp removal with slight bleeding. Hand files used to negotiate canal to size 15, working length measured at 21mm. Canal preparation with nickel-titanium instruments using Glyde. Irrigation, drying, trial fit of master cone to working length, final irrigation, drying, root canal filling with SP and gutta-percha, base placement, resin restoration. Schedule for final restoration.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151) with pigmentation. Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: General oral environment with calculus (++), pigmentation (+++), and gingival redness in the anterior region.

Treatment Recommendations: Regular scaling and dental check-ups.

Plaque disclosing agent was applied, followed by rinsing, radiographs, steam eye mask application, air polishing, full-mouth supragingival scaling, enamel sealing to prevent caries, and iodine glycerin application. Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with regular use of mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Examination: Tooth 42 is missing with well-healed gingiva and sufficient keratinized gingiva. General oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 42 shows adequate bone volume and quality.
Diagnosis: Tooth 42, dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prosthesis for tooth 42.
Management: The patient was fully informed and consented to the treatment plan, signing the informed consent for oral implant restoration. Prophylactic antibiotics were administered preoperatively. Routine disinfection and draping were performed, followed by 4% articaine local infiltration anesthesia. A horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge crest, full-thickness flap reflection, site preparation to the planned depth, and placement of a 3.3x10mm TI implant with a 3.6x5mm healing abutment. Tension-free suturing was completed, and radiographic imaging confirmed proper positioning.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs. Bite on gauze for 40 minutes, avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours, and consume food after 2 hours, avoiding hot foods.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect.
Examination: Tooth 37 has a mesiodistal gap of 8.5mm and an occlusal-gingival distance of 5mm. The alveolar ridge is resorbed, and there is no overeruption of opposing teeth. Overall oral hygiene is fair, with calculus and staining present. No significant abnormalities in occlusion or joints.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 37 shows an alveolar bone width of 8mm and height of 12mm.
Diagnosis: Tooth 37: Dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was fully informed and consented to the treatment, signing the informed consent for oral implant restoration. After routine disinfection and draping, local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine was administered. A horizontal incision was made at the crest of the alveolar ridge, full-thickness flap elevation was performed, and the site was prepared to the predetermined depth. A Neoss implant, size 4.5x9 mm, was placed and covered with a screw. Tension-free suturing was performed, and a radiograph confirmed the position.
Medical Instructions: Sutures to be removed in 1-2 weeks.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect.
Examination: Tooth 37 has a mesiodistal gap of 8.5mm and an occlusal-gingival distance of 5mm, with an alveolar ridge depression and no overeruption of opposing teeth. Overall oral hygiene is fair with calculus and staining present. No significant occlusal or joint abnormalities observed.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 37 shows an alveolar bone width of 8mm and height of 12mm.
Diagnosis: Tooth 37, dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Informed consent obtained for dental implant restoration. Under local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine, a horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge crest, full-thickness flap reflected, and osteotomy prepared to the planned depth. A Neoss implant (4.5x9 mm) was placed with cover screw, tension-free suturing performed, and postoperative radiograph taken.
Medical Instructions: Suture removal in 1-2 weeks.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect.
Examination: Teeth 15, 44, and 46 are missing, with well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized gingiva, and good alveolar ridge form without significant depression. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint function. Teeth 26, 34, and 36 have sutures in place with well-healed wounds.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 15 and 46 show adequate bone volume and quality. Tooth 44 shows adequate bone quality but insufficient volume.
Diagnosis: Teeth 15, 26, 34, 36, 44, and 46: dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prostheses for teeth 15, 44, and 46. Suture removal for teeth 34 and 36.
Management: For teeth 15, 44, and 46, informed consent was obtained, and the patient signed the consent form for implant restoration. After routine disinfection, local infiltration anesthesia with 4% articaine was administered. Circular incisions were made at the crest of the alveolar ridge for tooth 15, and horizontal incisions for teeth 44 and 46. Full-thickness flaps were elevated, and osteotomies were prepared to the planned depth. A BEGO implant (3.75x10mm) was placed in tooth 15 with a healing abutment (4.5x5mm), and BEGO implants (3.75x10mm and 4.5x10mm) were placed in teeth 44 and 46 with cover screws. Tension-free suturing was performed, and radiographic verification was satisfactory. Sutures were removed from teeth 34 and 36 after disinfection.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Examination: Teeth 14 and 16 show well-healed gingiva at the missing sites, with adequate keratinized gingiva. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 14 and 16 show insufficient bone volume but acceptable bone quality.
Diagnosis: Dental arch defect (K08.100x002) on teeth 14, 15, and 16.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prosthesis for teeth 14 and 16.
Management: The doctor informed the patient of the procedure, and the patient provided informed consent by signing the 'Oral Implant Restoration Informed Consent Form.' Preoperative prophylactic antibiotics were administered. Routine disinfection was performed, and local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge crest, and a full-thickness flap was elevated. Osteotomy was performed to the planned depth. A Dentium implant, size 4.5x8mm, was placed at site 14 with a 4.5L healing abutment, and another Dentium implant, size 4.5x8mm, was placed at site 16 with a 5.5L healing abutment. Bone grafting was performed on the buccal side, covered with a membrane. Tension-free suturing was done, and a postoperative radiograph was taken to confirm position.
Medical Instructions: Advise the patient to follow up if discomfort occurs. Bite on gauze for 40 minutes, avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours, and start eating 2 hours post-surgery. Recommend avoiding overly hot foods.

*   **Occlusal Adjustment:** Fine-tuning of the bite relationship to ensure comfort and proper load distribution.

**Medical Instructions:**
1.  Maintain excellent oral hygiene, including gentle brushing and flossing around the implant sites.
2.  Avoid excessive force or biting on the new prosthesis initially.
3.  Schedule the next follow-up appointment as per the established schedule for monitoring healing and function.

**Remarks:**
The patient is in the critical phase of prosthetic rehabilitation. The success of the final outcome heavily relies on the accuracy of the initial planning and the precision of the prosthetic fitting performed during this visit. All necessary pre-operative and post-operative protocols must be strictly followed.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect (K08.100x002). Treatment: implant-supported denture restoration.
Examination: Tooth 37 site shows well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized gingiva, good alveolar ridge form, no significant depression, and adequate gingival-occlusal distance. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 37 site shows adequate bone volume and quality.
Diagnosis: Tooth 37: Dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: Tooth 37: Implant-supported denture restoration.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 37, the patient was fully informed and consented to the procedure. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Under local anesthesia, a horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge, full-thickness flap elevation was performed, and the implant site was prepared. An IT implant (4.1x10 mm) was placed, and a cover screw was inserted. Tension-free suturing was done, and a postoperative radiograph was taken.
Medical Instructions: Follow up if discomfort occurs. Bite on gauze for 40 minutes, avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours, and resume eating after 2 hours. Avoid hot foods.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect.
Examination: Teeth 15, 44, and 46 are missing, with well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized tissue, and good alveolar ridge form. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint function. Teeth 26, 34, and 36 with sutures in place and good wound healing.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 15 and 46 show adequate bone volume and quality. Tooth 44 shows adequate bone quality but insufficient bone volume.
Diagnosis: Teeth 15, 26, 34, 36, 44, 46, dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prostheses for teeth 15, 44, and 46. Suture removal for teeth 34 and 36.
Management: For teeth 15, 44, and 46, the patient was fully informed and consented to the treatment plan, signing the informed consent for oral implant restoration. Under local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine, a circular incision was made at the crest of the alveolar ridge for tooth 15, and horizontal incisions for teeth 44 and 46. Full-thickness flaps were raised, and osteotomies were prepared to the planned depth. A BEGO implant (3.75x10mm) was placed in tooth 15 with a healing abutment (4.5x5mm), a BEGO implant (3.75x10mm) in tooth 44 with a cover screw, and a BEGO implant (4.5x10mm) in tooth 46 with a cover screw. Tension-free suturing was performed, and radiographs confirmed correct positioning. Sutures were removed from teeth 34 and 36 after disinfection.
Medical Instructions: Follow up if any discomfort arises. Avoid brushing or rinsing the mouth for 24 hours. Begin eating 2 hours post-procedure, avoiding hot foods.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Partial edentulism (K08.100x002). Treatment: Implant placement.
Examination: Teeth 45, 46, and 47 are missing. The gingiva and occlusion are normal, and adjacent teeth show no abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Partial edentulism of teeth 45, 46, and 47 (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: Implant placement for teeth 45 and 47.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 45 and 47, the patient was fully informed and consented to the procedure by signing the informed consent for oral implant restoration. Preoperative antibiotics were administered, and routine disinfection was performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was applied. A horizontal incision was made at the alveolar crest, full-thickness flap reflection was performed, and implant sites were prepared to the planned depth. BEGO implants were placed: 474.5*8 mm and 453.75*10 mm, with cover screws. Tension-free suturing was done, and postoperative radiographs confirmed the position.
Medical Instructions: Follow up if any discomfort arises. Bite on gauze for 40 minutes. Avoid brushing and rinsing for 24 hours. Eating is allowed after 2 hours, but avoid hot foods.

Oral hygiene education was provided.
Medical Instructions: The patient is advised to rinse and drink water after half an hour. Avoid spicy, acidic, cold foods for three days. Refrain from smoking, and avoid coffee, tea, cola, and other dark-colored drinks for a week. Regular dental cleanings are recommended. Follow up if any discomfort arises.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect.
Examination: Teeth 16 and 41 are missing, with a mesiodistal gap of 9mm and an occlusogingival distance of 5mm. The alveolar ridge is resorbed with no overeruption of opposing teeth. Oral hygiene is fair with calculus (+) and pigmentation (+). No significant occlusal or joint abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 16 and 41 show alveolar bone width of 8mm and height of 10mm.
Diagnosis: Dental arch defect at teeth 16 and 41.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 16, the patient was fully informed and consented to the oral implant restoration procedure. Standard disinfection and draping were performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine was administered. A horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge crest, and a full-thickness flap was elevated. The site was prepared to the planned depth, and a Bego implant (size 4.1x10 mm) was placed with a cover screw. Tension-free suturing was performed, and radiographic verification of placement was done. For tooth 41, an impression was taken for a Maryland bridge design.
Medical Instructions: Sutures to be removed in 1-2 weeks.
Remarks:

**Patient Information:**

*   **Gender:** Female

*   **Present Illness:** Patient reports bleeding while brushing for several months without pain or discomfort.
*   **Past Medical History:** Denies history of hypertension, heart disease, or diabetes. No known allergies to penicillin, metals, or other substances. Reports long-term consumption of coffee and other staining beverages.

---

**Patient Record:**
*   **Visit Type:** Follow-up
*   **Preliminary Diagnosis:** Gingivitis/Periodontitis (K05.5) related to plaque accumulation and potential staining.

**Examination:**
*   **Soft Tissues:** Examination of the gingiva reveals generalized mild to moderate gingival inflammation (erythema and slight edema) noted throughout the oral cavity, consistent with chronic plaque accumulation. Bleeding upon gentle probing was noted, correlating with the patient's chief complaint. Mucosa appears generally intact, though some staining is present.
*   **Hard Tissues (Teeth):** Visual inspection of the visible teeth shows generalized extrinsic staining, likely due to long-term consumption of coffee and staining beverages, as reported by the patient. No acute caries or significant structural defects were immediately apparent in the visible areas.

**Radiographic Examination:**
*   *No specific radiographic images were provided for this report.*

**Diagnosis:**
*   **Gingivitis (K05.5):** Generalized inflammation of the gingiva due to chronic plaque accumulation.
*   **Extrinsic Staining:** Generalized discoloration of tooth surfaces due to dietary factors (coffee/staining beverages).

**Treatment Plan:**
1.  **Scaling and Root Planing (SRP):** Comprehensive professional cleaning to remove calculus and plaque from the gingival sulcus to reduce inflammation and bleeding.
2.  **Oral Hygiene Instruction (OHI):** Intensive instruction on proper brushing, flossing techniques, and dietary modifications to manage plaque control and staining.
3.  **Staining Management:** Counseling on reducing intake of highly staining beverages.

**Treatment Recommendations:**
1.  **Maintenance:** Patient must adhere strictly to a rigorous daily oral hygiene routine (brushing twice daily, flossing daily).
2.  **Follow-up:** Re-evaluation in 3-4 weeks to assess the response to scaling and the control of gingival inflammation.
3.  **Dietary Modification:** Encourage reduction in coffee and other staining beverages to minimize future aesthetic concerns.

**Management:**
*   **Scaling and Root Planing (SRP):** Performed using ultrasonic scaling and manual curettes to thoroughly debride the gingival surfaces and root surfaces.
*   **Prophylaxis:** Application of a fluoride varnish to help strengthen enamel and reduce susceptibility to staining.
*   **Patient Education:** Detailed instruction provided regarding the etiology of gum bleeding, the importance of mechanical plaque removal, and personalized techniques for effective home care.

**Medical Instructions:**
1.  **Brushing Technique:** Use a soft-bristled toothbrush and employ gentle, circular motions, focusing on the gum line.
2.  **Flossing:** Floss at least once daily to remove plaque between teeth.

**Diagnosis:**
*   **Final Diagnosis:** Chronic Gingivitis.
*   **Affected Tooth Numbers:** Generalized gingival inflammation noted across multiple teeth.
*   **ICD-10 Codes:** K05.1.

**Treatment Plan:**
1.  **Phase 1 (Current Visit):** Comprehensive periodontal assessment, thorough patient education on oral hygiene techniques, and initiation of a detailed plaque control regimen.
2.  **Phase 2 (Follow-up):** Re-evaluation in 4-6 weeks to assess the response to the initial treatment plan and monitor for resolution of gingival inflammation.

**Treatment Recommendations:**
1.  **Oral Hygiene Instruction:** Intensive instruction on proper brushing technique (soft brush, gentle circular motions), flossing technique, and tongue cleaning.
2.  **Dietary Modification:** Advise the patient to moderate consumption of highly acidic beverages (tea and coffee) and to rinse the mouth with water after consuming them.
3.  **Professional Cleaning:** Recommend a professional scaling and root planing (SRP) appointment if gingivitis does not resolve within 4 weeks of diligent home care.

*   **Materials and Techniques Used:** Standard clinical instruments, periodontal probes, and educational materials.

**Medical Instructions:**
1.  **Enhanced Oral Hygiene:** Brush twice daily for two minutes using a soft-bristled toothbrush.
2.  **Flossing:** Floss once daily to remove plaque between teeth.
3.  **Rinsing:** Rinse mouth thoroughly with warm salt water after meals and after consuming tea or coffee.
4.  **Follow-up:** Schedule a follow-up appointment in 4-6 weeks for re-evaluation.
5.  **Symptom Monitoring:** Report any increase in bleeding, pain, swelling, or changes in gum color immediately.

**Remarks:**
The patient's chief complaint of gingival bleeding is highly suggestive of chronic gingivitis, likely exacerbated by plaque accumulation, potentially influenced by the long-term consumption of tea and coffee. Strict adherence to the prescribed oral hygiene regimen is crucial for successful management and resolution of the condition.

Treatment Recommendations:

An Osstem 5.0*8.5mm bone-level implant was placed in site #17, and a cover screw was tightened. The area was sutured tightly.
Medical Instructions: Follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 16 (K04.000).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 16, the temporary seal was removed, medication retrieved, and the pulp chamber opened. Three canals were located, with slight bleeding. Hand files were used to negotiate the canals to size 15. Working lengths were measured: palatal canal 21mm, buccal canals 18mm. With Glyde assistance, rotary nickel-titanium instruments were used for canal preparation. The canals were irrigated, dried, medicated, and temporarily sealed.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects. Return for follow-up in one week.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: Generalized gingival redness and swelling with easy bleeding upon probing, no periodontal pockets detected. Presence of calculus (+) and pigmentation (+).
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the diagnosis, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Air polishing, full-mouth supragingival scaling, and polishing were performed. Enamel sealing for caries prevention, application of steam eye mask, iodine glycerin, and oral hygiene education were provided.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Refrain from consuming coffee, tea, cola, and other pigmented beverages. No smoking. Maintain oral hygiene and use mouthwash. Schedule regular follow-ups and return if symptoms persist.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Teeth 14, 24, 26, and 36 have occlusal caries extending into the middle dentin layer, negative to probing, cold, heat, and percussion tests.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in teeth 14, 24, 26, and 36 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Restoration.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Rubber dam isolation was used, caries were removed, and cavities were prepared. Calcium hydroxide was placed near the pulp, followed by etching, rinsing, drying, bonding agent application, and light curing. Composite resin was incrementally placed and light cured, with final shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Follow-up if discomfort occurs. Avoid hard foods.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Full mouth plaque management and caries restoration.

Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth 16 and 36 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Full mouth plaque management and caries restoration for teeth 16 and 36.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Oral hygiene instruction was provided, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use. Caries gel was applied to soften carious tissue, which was then removed, cavities were prepared, etched with 35% acid, bonded, and filled with VOCO resin, restoring occlusal anatomy and polished.
Medical Instructions: Avoid sticky and hard foods for 24 hours. Return if discomfort occurs.
Remarks: Full mouth cleaning and fluoride application were discussed. The guardian is considering pit and fissure sealants for eight teeth and will decide after consulting elsewhere.

Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth 17, 27, 37, and 47 (K02.800x007).

Management: For teeth 17, 27, 37, and 47, the patient was informed of the diagnosis, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Rubber dam isolation was used, caries were removed, and cavities were prepared. After cleaning and drying, calcium hydroxide was placed near the pulp. Acid etching, rinsing, drying, bonding agent application, and light curing were performed. Resin was layered and light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Single arch edentulism (K08.101). Maxillary arch edentulism. Treatment: Maxillary ALL-ON-6 implant-supported prosthesis.
Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26 are missing. The gingiva at the edentulous sites is healthy, with good alveolar ridge form and adequate gingival-occlusal distance. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement. No other abnormalities noted.
Radiographic Examination: Adequate bone volume and quality observed for teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26.
Diagnosis: Single arch edentulism (K08.101). Maxillary arch edentulism.
Treatment Plan: Placement of ALL-ON-6 implant-supported prosthesis for teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26, the patient was fully informed and consented by signing the oral implant restoration consent form. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Standard disinfection and draping were performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made along the alveolar ridge, followed by a full-thickness flap reflection. Implant sites were prepared to the planned depth, and Dentium implants were placed: 3.4*12mm for teeth 11, 21; 3.8*12mm for teeth 14, 24; 4.3*10mm for teeth 16, 26. Abutments were placed on teeth 11, 21, 14, 24, and cover screws on teeth 16, 26. Bone grafting and membrane coverage were performed. Tension-free suturing was completed, and postoperative radiographs confirmed implant positioning. The procedure lasted from 10:20 to 11:40. Impressions were taken for transitional prosthesis fabrication, and occlusion was adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: Strictly follow postoperative implant care instructions. Take oral antibiotics as prescribed. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Suture removal in two weeks.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect (K08.100×002). Treatment: Implant restoration.
Examination: Tooth 46: Missing with a mesiodistal gap of 8.5mm and an occlusal-gingival distance of 5mm. Alveolar ridge is resorbed, no elongation of opposing teeth. Overall oral hygiene is fair with calculus and staining present. No significant abnormalities in occlusion or joints.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 46: Alveolar bone width of 8mm and height of 12mm.
Diagnosis: Tooth 46: Dental arch defect (K08.100×002).
Treatment Plan: Implant restoration for tooth 46.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Tooth 46: The patient was fully informed and consented to the oral implant restoration procedure. Preoperative antibiotics were administered, and standard disinfection was performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made at the crest of the alveolar ridge, full-thickness flap was elevated, and the site was prepared to the planned depth. A Nobel implant (5.0x10mm) was placed, and a healing abutment was attached. Tension-free suturing was done, and radiographic verification was satisfactory.
Medical Instructions: Strictly follow post-implant care instructions, take oral antibiotics, and return for follow-up if any discomfort occurs. Suture removal in two weeks.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental arch defect (K08.100x002).
Examination: Tooth 36 is missing with a mesiodistal gap of 8.5mm and an occlusogingival distance of 5mm. The alveolar ridge is resorbed with no elongation of the opposing tooth. Overall oral hygiene is acceptable with calculus and staining present. No significant occlusal or joint abnormalities. Tooth 31 is also missing with a mesiodistal gap of 4.1mm and a resorbed alveolar ridge, with no elongation of the opposing tooth.
Radiographic Examination: For tooth 36, the alveolar bone width is 8mm and height is 12mm.
Diagnosis: Dental arch defect for teeth 31 and 36 (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 36, the patient was fully informed of the procedure and signed the informed consent for oral implant restoration. Preoperative antibiotics were administered, and standard disinfection was performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made at the alveolar ridge crest, followed by full-thickness flap elevation. The site was prepared, and a Zimmer implant (4.1x11.5mm) was placed with a cover screw. Tension-free suturing was performed, and a postoperative radiograph confirmed the position. Procedure time: 14:25 to 14:45. For tooth 31, an intraoral scan was taken for the fabrication of a temporary adhesive bridge.
Medical Instructions: Strictly follow post-implant surgery care instructions. Take oral antibiotics as prescribed. Return for suture removal in two weeks.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Maxillary Kennedy Class IV edentulous space.
Examination: Teeth 11 and 21 are missing. Oral hygiene is average, with a low and flat alveolar ridge. Occlusion and overjet are normal.
Radiographic Examination: Tooth 21 alveolar ridge height is 13.27mm and width is 12.99mm.
Diagnosis: Teeth 11 and 21, maxillary Kennedy Class IV edentulous space.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant placement for tooth 21.
Management: For tooth 21, under local anesthesia with Bilocaine, a flap was raised from teeth 11 to 21. The site was prepared with sequential drilling, and an ICX 3.8x10 mm implant was placed with 50N-cm torque. Bone grafting with membrane was applied, and a cover screw was placed. For tooth 11, palatal soft tissue was grafted to the labial side, sutured, and hemostasis was achieved with gauze pressure.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect (K08.100×002). Treatment: implant-supported denture restoration.
Examination: Tooth 46 is missing, with well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized gingiva, good alveolar ridge form, no significant depression, and good gingival-occlusal distance. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement.
Radiographic Examination: Adequate bone volume and quality at site of missing tooth 46.
Diagnosis: Dental arch defect at site of tooth 46 (K08.100×002).
Treatment Plan: Implant-supported denture restoration for tooth 46.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed of the procedure and provided informed consent for oral implant restoration. Prophylactic antibiotics were administered preoperatively. Standard disinfection and draping were performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was administered. A horizontal incision was made on the alveolar ridge, full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap elevated, site prepared to the planned depth, and a NOBEL implant (5.0*10 mm) was placed with cover screw. Tension-free suturing was performed, and postoperative radiographs confirmed correct positioning.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Bite on gauze for 40 minutes, avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours, and refrain from eating for 2 hours post-surgery. Avoid hot foods and drinks.

Bone grafting and membrane coverage were performed, and tension-free suturing was completed. A postoperative radiograph confirmed the implant position.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Partial edentulism (K08.100x002).
Examination: Teeth 34, 35, and 36 are missing with a mesiodistal gap of 25.5 mm and an occlusogingival distance of 5 mm. The alveolar ridge is resorbed with no supraeruption of opposing teeth. Overall oral hygiene is fair with presence of calculus and staining. No significant occlusal or joint abnormalities noted.
Radiographic Examination: Radiographs show alveolar bone width of 5.1 mm and height of 11 mm in the area of missing teeth 34, 35, and 36.
Diagnosis: Partial edentulism of teeth 34, 35, and 36 (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Informed consent for implant restoration was obtained. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Local anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made at the crest of the ridge, and a full-thickness flap was reflected. Osteotomy sites were prepared, and Dentium implants were placed: 3.4x10 mm for site 34 and 4.3x8 mm for site 36. Cover screws were placed, bone graft material was applied, and the flap was sutured with tension-free closure. Postoperative radiographs confirmed implant positioning.
Medical Instructions: Strict adherence to post-implant care instructions is required. Oral antibiotics should be taken as prescribed. Follow-up for suture removal in two weeks.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in primary teeth (K02.101). Treatment: Resin filling.
Examination: Poor overall oral hygiene with plaque accumulation at the cervical areas and carious lesions present. No tenderness on probing, cold, or percussion tests. Gingiva appears normal.

Diagnosis: Moderate caries in primary teeth (K02.101).
Treatment Plan: Resin filling for the affected teeth.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Caries gel was applied to soften the carious tissue, which was then removed with excavation tools. The cavity was prepared, etched with 35% phosphoric acid, and a bonding agent was applied. 3M Z350 resin was used to restore the occlusal anatomy, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing. Teeth were cleaned, and fluoride varnish was applied to the entire mouth.
Medical Instructions: Oral hygiene education was provided, including instructions on brushing, flossing, and using interdental brushes.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: pulpitis (K04.005).
Examination: Tooth 45 is temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis of tooth 45 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 45, isolation was applied, temporary seal was removed, medicament was retrieved, the pulp chamber was accessed, and one canal was located. Pulp extirpation was incomplete with slight bleeding. Hand files were used to negotiate the canal to size 15, with a working length of 19mm. Canal preparation was done using NiTi rotary instruments with Glyde assistance. The canal was irrigated, dried, and the master cone was trial-fitted to working length. The canal was obturated with SP and gutta-percha, a base was placed, and the access was temporarily sealed with GIC.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

Examination: Tooth 45 is temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 45 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 45, isolation was achieved, the temporary seal and medicament were removed, and the pulp chamber was accessed. One canal was located, and the pulp was partially removed with slight bleeding. Hand files were used to negotiate the canal to size 15, and the working length was measured at 19mm. With Glyde assistance, rotary nickel-titanium instruments were used to prepare the canal. The canal was irrigated, cleaned, and dried. The master cone was trial-fitted to the working length, and the canal was cleaned and dried again. The canal was filled with SP and gutta-percha, a base was placed, and the tooth was temporarily sealed with GIC.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x008). Treatment: Inlay restoration and resin filling for tooth 15.
Examination: Teeth 15 and 16 show brown cavitated lesions on the occlusal surfaces, with tooth 16 probing to soft dentin and extending more than half the buccolingual width. Cold, percussion, and mobility tests are negative. Periodontal tissues appear normal. No elongation of opposing teeth. Other teeth and occlusion appear normal. Tooth 25 is temporarily sealed with some soft debris accumulation.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Deep caries in permanent teeth 15 and 16 (K02.800x008).
Treatment Plan: Inlay restoration for tooth 16 and resin filling for tooth 15.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 16, the patient was informed about various restorative materials, costs, time, potential follow-up visits, and complications. The patient opted for an all-ceramic crown. Caries removal and inlay preparation were performed, followed by precise finishing and impression taking with silicone and alginate. Occlusal records and shade matching (2L1.5) were completed. For tooth 25, the temporary crown and provisional material were removed, abutment and restoration cleaned, and the restoration was tried in for fit and proximal contact. It was cemented with dual-cure resin cement, occlusion adjusted, and polished. For tooth 15, caries removal, cavity preparation, etching, drying, bonding agent application, and resin filling were performed, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard objects, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent tooth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Tooth 47 has a temporary crown, with no abnormalities in the soft tissues. Teeth 25 and 26 show occlusal caries extending to the middle dentin layer, with negative responses to probing, cold, and heat tests. Tooth 37 is temporarily sealed, with slight gingival redness.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries in permanent teeth 25 and 26 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Restoration for teeth 25 and 26.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. For tooth 47, the temporary crown and sealing material were removed, the abutment and restoration were cleaned, and the restoration was tried in for fit. The patient was satisfied, and dual-cure resin cement was used for cementation, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing. For teeth 25 and 26, rubber dam isolation was applied, caries removal and cavity preparation were performed, and calcium hydroxide was placed near the pulp. Acid etching, bonding agent application, and layered resin filling were completed, followed by occlusal adjustment and polishing. For tooth 37, the temporary seal was removed, and resin filling was applied, with a three-month follow-up scheduled to assess apical healing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Chronic pulpitis (K04.003).
Examination: Teeth 14 and 15 exhibit cervical wedge defects with pulp exposure. The mucosa in the apical region is normal, and both teeth show positive percussion.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 14 and 15 show wedge defects with pulp exposure.
Diagnosis: Chronic pulpitis of teeth 14 and 15 (K04.003).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 14 and 15, rubber dam isolation was applied, and temporary seals were removed. The pulp chamber was accessed, and the canal orifices were explored. Pulp extirpation was performed. GG drills were used to prepare the coronal third of the canal, and an 08# C file was used to determine the canal length, measured at 19mm with an apex locator. Canals were prepared using a large taper technique and machine files to F2. Ultrasonic irrigation with 1% sodium hypochlorite and distilled water was performed, alternating with saline, and the canals were dried. Calcium hydroxide paste was placed as a temporary seal.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and attend scheduled follow-ups.

Treatment Recommendations:

Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Return if any discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Pulpitis. Treatment: Root canal therapy.
Examination: Tooth 24 shows ink-like discoloration on the distal proximal surface, with probe retention reaching deep dentin, and sensitivity to cold stimuli.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 24.
Treatment Plan: Root canal therapy for tooth 24.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Tooth 24: The patient was informed about the condition and consented to treatment. Under rubber dam isolation, caries removal was performed using high-speed handpiece and slow-speed round bur, followed by pulp chamber opening, pulp extirpation, and canal negotiation. The canals were irrigated with saline, and canal length was determined (17, 19 mm) using an electronic apex locator. Nickel-titanium rotary files were used to prepare the canals to size F2. The canals were disinfected with 2.5% sodium hypochlorite and distilled water, and a trial fit of the master cone was taken. After irrigation, drying, and isolation, the canals were dried with paper points and obturated using SP and gutta-percha with vertical compaction. A post-operative radiograph confirmed proper filling. Post space preparation was done, and a fiber post was bonded. The tooth was prepared and refined for a crown. A silicone impression and bite registration were taken, and a temporary crown was placed.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if any discomfort occurs.

Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in teeth 13 and 14 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 13 and 14, temporary sealing was removed, root canals were cleaned, and ultrasonic irrigation with 1% sodium hypochlorite and distilled water was performed. Master cone radiographs were taken, and main cones were disinfected for use. Canals were dried with paper points, and filled with sealer and gutta-percha points. Post-operative radiographs confirmed proper fill. A fiber post was placed in tooth 14, occlusion adjusted, and polished. Teeth 13 and 14 were prepared, impressions and occlusal records taken, and temporary crowns fabricated and placed.
Medical Instructions: Return if any discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and attend scheduled follow-ups.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries. Treatment: Restoration.
Examination: Teeth [4M][1D] show ink-like discoloration with intact marginal ridges. Caries extend to deep dentin, negative to probing; cold test is variable; no mobility; gingiva appears normal.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth [4M][1D] show low-density shadows near the pulp, with no periapical abnormalities.
Diagnosis: Deep caries on teeth [4M][1D].
Treatment Plan: Restoration for teeth [4M][1D].
Treatment Recommendations: Restoration for teeth [4M][1D].
Management: Rubber dam isolation was used for teeth [4M][1D]. Caries were removed chemically, cavities were prepared, and acid etching was performed. After rinsing and drying, a light-cured base was placed, bonding agent applied and thinned, and light-cured. 3M-P60 resin was layered and cured incrementally, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the treated side for 2 hours. Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.
Remarks: Next appointment for restoration of tooth 46; caries on teeth 22 and 27 not yet addressed.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Pulpitis (K04.005).
Examination: Teeth 13 and 14 are temporarily sealed, with normal mucosa in the periapical area and no percussion sensitivity.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in teeth 13 and 14 (K04.005).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 13 and 14, temporary seals were removed, and the root canals were cleaned with 1% sodium hypochlorite and distilled water using ultrasonic agitation. Master cone fit was verified with a trial radiograph, and the main cone was sterilized for use. The canals were dried with paper points, and obturation was completed using sealer and gutta-percha cones. A post-operative radiograph confirmed proper filling. For tooth 14, a fiber post was placed, occlusion adjusted, and polished. Both teeth were prepared for crowns, impressions and bite registrations were taken, and temporary crowns were fabricated and placed.
Medical Instructions: Return if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and attend follow-up appointments as scheduled.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Moderate caries of permanent teeth (K02.800x007). Treatment: Filling procedure.
Examination: Teeth 26 and 36 show occlusal caries on tooth 16 and buccal caries on tooth 36, reaching the middle layer of dentin. Sensitivity tests for cold, heat, and percussion are negative.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Moderate caries of teeth 26 and 36 (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: Filling procedure.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 26 and 36, a rubber dam was placed, caries were removed, and cavities were prepared. The cavities were cleaned and dried, and calcium hydroxide was placed near the pulp. Acid etching was followed by rinsing and drying. A bonding agent was applied, thinned, and light-cured. 3M resin was layered and light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Avoid chewing on the affected side for 2 hours. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Avoid biting hard objects.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: secondary caries (K02.800x001).
Examination: Tooth 36 shows loss of temporary material, with normal mucosa in the apical area and no significant gingival swelling. Percussion test is negative.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Secondary caries in tooth 36 (K02.800x001).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 36, the tooth surface was polished, and the inlay was tried in. The inlay and tooth surface were treated with hydrofluoric acid etching for 90 seconds, rinsed, and dried. Ultrasonic cleaning with 95% alcohol for 90 seconds was performed, followed by drying. Silane coupling agent was applied and dried with hot air for 20 seconds. The tooth was etched, rinsed, dried, and isolated. Both the tooth and inlay were coated with bonding agent (Ivoclar Vivadent Variolink N system), and the inlay was cemented with dual-cure resin cement, followed by light curing. Occlusion was adjusted, polished, and photographed.
Medical Instructions: Advise the patient to avoid consuming excessively cold, hot, hard, or sticky foods. If any loosening or discomfort occurs, return for follow-up, scheduling an appointment by phone in advance.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries.
Examination: Tooth 47 shows a distal occlusal discoloration with probe retention and sensitivity. Cold stimulus causes sharp pain, and the lesion extends to deep dentin.
Radiographic Examination: Radiograph shows carious lesion near the pulp in tooth 47.
Diagnosis: Deep caries in tooth 47.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 47, local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine was administered. Carious dentin was removed to deep dentin without pulp exposure. A proximal restoration system was used, pulp protection and base placement were done, and the cavity was filled with resin. The abutment was prepared and refined. A silicone impression and occlusal record were taken for a custom inlay restoration, and the tooth was temporarily sealed. The patient was informed that root canal therapy might be needed if pain develops.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. If symptoms develop, return for root canal therapy.

Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 17, 27, 36, 46, and 47, rubber dam was placed, caries removed, cavity prepared, cleaned, and dried. Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901).
Examination: Teeth 36 and 46 show black carious lesions in buccal pits and fissures. Tooth 46 has a large occlusal restoration with poor marginal adaptation, probe catches, negative cold test, and no percussion sensitivity.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Dental caries on teeth 36 and 46 (K02.901).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 36 and 46, the patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. The existing restorations and carious tissue were removed, cavities were prepared, and caries gel was applied to soften the decayed tissue. Decay was removed, cavities cleaned and dried, base placed, etched, rinsed, and dried. Bonding agent was applied, thinned, and light-cured. Resin was incrementally filled and light-cured, followed by shaping, occlusal adjustment, and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene, avoid hard or sticky foods, and return if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Tooth 16: Temporary seal removed, medicament retrieved, access opened, three canals located with slight bleeding. Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Return for follow-up in one week.

Diagnosis: Chronic pulpitis of tooth 26.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 26, the patient was informed of the diagnosis, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Local infiltration anesthesia with 4% Articaine was administered. Caries removal was incomplete, resulting in pulp exposure with significant bleeding. The pulp exposure was enlarged, and a non-arsenic devitalizing agent was applied, followed by temporary sealing with Cervitron.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Return for follow-up in one week.

Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901). Treatment: Filling.
Examination: Tooth 36 shows black discoloration at the buccal pit, not reaching the dentin layer. The probe catches slightly, but there is no percussion, probing, or cold sensitivity. Gingiva appears normal.
Radiographic Examination: No significant radiolucency observed on tooth 36.
Diagnosis: Dental caries (K02.901) on tooth 36.
Treatment Plan: Filling for tooth 36.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis were explained to the child and parents, who consented to the treatment. Caries removal was performed, cavity preparation was completed, caries gel was applied to soften the carious tissue, and decay was removed with instruments. The cavity was cleaned and dried, etched, rinsed, and dried again. A bonding agent was applied, thinned, and cured with light. Resin was layered and cured incrementally, shaped, occlusion adjusted, and polished.
Medical Instructions: Maintain oral hygiene, avoid hard or sticky foods, and return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Adjusted occlusion and polished. Provided oral hygiene instruction, including brushing and flossing techniques. Applied fluoride varnish after plaque disclosure and polishing.
Medical Instructions: Educate on oral hygiene, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Remarks: Consider preventive or restorative treatment for teeth 17, 16, 26, 27, 37, and 47 at the next visit.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Secondary caries; deep caries. Treatment: Composite resin restoration.
Examination: Teeth 43, 44, 45, and 46 exhibit dark carious lesions at the cervical area extending to deep dentin, with cold sensitivity in teeth 43 and 46.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 44 and 45 show adequate root canal fillings. Teeth 43 and 46 have deep caries.
Diagnosis: Teeth 44 and 45: Secondary caries. Teeth 43 and 46: Deep caries.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 43 and 46, caries removal to deep dentin, pulp protection, base placement, and composite resin restoration were performed. Occlusal adjustment and polishing were done. The patient was informed that root canal therapy might be needed if postoperative pain occurs. For teeth 44 and 45, abutment preparation and refinement were completed, and silicone impressions and bite registrations were taken for temporary crown restoration.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs.
